title: acer campestre f. hederifolium
intro: Acer campestre f. hederifolium, also known as the Maple-leaved Field Maple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, and is believed to offer health benefits like reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Acer campestre f. hederifolium include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves or bark in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
